titleStmt.title: Letter from James Robert Bargrave Armstrong to his father, Henry Bruce Armstrong, 22 July 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: James Robert Bargrave Armstrong

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
This letter is written from the front by 'Robin', James Robert Bargrave Armstrong (1893-1980) to his father, Henry Bruce Armstrong. Robin tells his father his location without actually naming it, instead saying that 'Forfo' had been there for some months. He mentions running into former acquaintances from WInchester and Cambridge, and says he had lunch with one of them at HQ, a 'very nice old chateau'. He suggests that H.B. may have known the father or grandfather of Robin's senior captain. He finishes with an account of the weather.
This letter is part of a series of correspondence belonging to Henry Bruce Armstrong (1844-1943). The correspondence largely concerns Armstrong’s estate and financial affairs with correspondents ranging from family members to high ranking Northern Irish politicians. At the time of this letter he was a County Councillor for Armagh, and he would later become a Westminster MP.
Robin served in the First World War, and later became a barrister.

msIdentifier.repository: Public Record Office of Northern Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
viiith Hussars
I.E.F
France 22/7/16 My dear father We are still in the same spot —
the same place where Forfor was
some months. We have now
got a few tents up & are fairly
comfortable. Of course we may
be off any time at an hour &
a half's notice I have come across 3 fellows
that I knew at Winchester &
Cambridge. Since I have been
here — one you remember
Hector Munro who is A.D.C.
to the infantry corps general.
I had lunch with him yesterday
at H.Q. at a very nice old
Chateau! — The senior captain in the regiment
is the Hon. R.N.D. Ryder. I suppose
it was probably his grandfather
that was at Trinity with you or
perhaps his father. We have had 3 lovely days
up till now but today it
is clouding over again & I'm
afraid shall have more rain. Your affectionate son Robin
